description: Deep admin operations dashboard for Tempo operators. Covers HTTP error analysis (4xx/5xx), span rejections, request volumes by route, latency percentiles, and write/query path component health.

panels.description: Percentage of all Tempo HTTP requests returning 4xx or 5xx status codes.
panels.title: Error Rate %

panels.description: Rate of HTTP 5xx (server error) responses per second from Tempo components.
panels.title: 5xx/sec

panels.description: Rate of HTTP 4xx (client error) responses per second from Tempo components.
panels.title: 4xx/sec

panels.description: Total HTTP request rate per second across all Tempo routes and status codes.
panels.title: Request Rate

panels.description: Percentage of all Tempo HTTP requests completing successfully (2xx/3xx).
panels.title: Success %

panels.description: 95th percentile request latency across all Tempo HTTP routes.
panels.title: P95 Latency

panels.description: Time series of error responses grouped by HTTP status code. Stacked view shows the composition of errors over time.
panels.title: Errors by HTTP Status Code

panels.description: Comparison of 5xx (server error) and 4xx (client error) rates as a percentage of total traffic over time.
panels.title: Error Rate % Over Time (5xx vs 4xx)

panels.description: Top 10 routes by 5xx (server error) rate. Identifies the most problematic server-side endpoints.
panels.title: Top 10 Routes -- 5xx Errors

panels.description: Top 10 routes by 4xx (client error) rate. Identifies endpoints receiving the most invalid or unauthorized requests.
panels.title: Top 10 Routes -- 4xx Errors

panels.description: Error rate (4xx + 5xx) broken down by cluster to isolate cluster-specific failures.
panels.title: Error Rate by Cluster

panels.description: Total request rate broken down by cluster. Useful for capacity and load distribution analysis.
panels.title: Request Rate by Cluster

panels.description: Rate of discarded (rejected) spans by reason. Common reasons include rate_limited, trace_too_large, and live_traces_exceeded.
panels.title: Discarded Spans by Reason

panels.description: Rate of HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests / rate limited) responses. Indicates clients being throttled by Tempo.
panels.title: Rate Limited (429) by Route

panels.description: Comparison of total spans received by distributors versus spans successfully ingested. The gap indicates rejected or dropped spans.
panels.title: Spans Received vs Accepted

panels.description: Top 15 routes ranked by total request volume. Identifies the most heavily used Tempo API endpoints.
panels.title: Top 15 Routes by Volume

panels.description: Request rate per route over time. Stacked view reveals traffic composition and shift patterns across routes.
panels.title: Request Rate by Route

panels.description: Error rate for write-path (push/ingest) routes. Monitors the health of span ingestion endpoints.
panels.title: Write Path Errors (Push Routes)

panels.description: Write-path error breakdown by HTTP status code. Shows the distribution of failure modes on ingestion.
panels.title: Write Errors by Status Code

panels.description: Error rate for query/search-path routes. Monitors the health of trace lookup and search endpoints.
panels.title: Query Path Errors (Search/Trace Routes)

panels.description: Query-path latency percentiles (P50, P90, P95, P99) for search and trace lookup routes.
panels.title: Query Latency Percentiles

panels.description: P95 latency ranked by route. Identifies the slowest Tempo API endpoints at the 95th percentile.
panels.title: P95 Latency by Route

panels.description: Global latency percentiles (P50, P90, P95, P99) across all Tempo HTTP routes over time.
panels.title: Latency Percentiles -- All Routes

title: Tempo Admin -- Errors, Rejections & Operations
